Pre-FOMC day - Intraday post (09/Dec)

Weekly post:

FOMC-Week, Positioning breakdown - Weekly post (08-12/Dec)

In the weekly post I wrote: “Monday is expected to be a cautiously malign day, market prepositions itself for the week, while Tuesday is hedging and waiting for the FOMC.” Here we are. Fed-cut odds exploded higher, tech vol premium mean-reverted, and we now have a tradable index on the AI factor.Structurally, until 6769.17 is held firmly and market doesn’t break it daily, the rebound continues. This is not too far from the 6755 ES futures CTA support.

Let’s look at the intraday positioning…

INTRADAY POST Coding today’s positioning:

This day is a clear fly pattern with its net center clustering at 6849.98, and with a net slightly negative speed profile.Customers are heavily buying upside vol, and modestly selling downside vol, however the vol of vol selling is very aggressive for tday ahead of FOMC. Market expects and tries to pin volatility, and set the market to rangebound, providing a path of the least resistance to the upside, in order to avoid any downside move. However, downside selling is not as confident for today, as it usually is.

Centroid is in the 6868.18 - 6887.17 area. And it is sticky towards its upper bound, margin of error of 6892.49 (this is a local pin)

Upside pivot is at 6870.26,very important level for today and a strong resistance after the first 1 hour.High and confident upside demand and low RV is needed to break it after.However it can be broken in the first 1 hour with no problem.

Downside pivot is at 6847.18,with primal target of 6825/30 area, that is a reversion target back to the center, especially if spot makes a high velocity move.But the max downside target is at 6792.47, that is a sticky spot.Here, again, in order to break through the 6825/30 zone, high put skew bid and low local RV is needed.

So, all in all, in the first hour the market can make its upside move, otherwise the upside pivot will cap the spot. If downside happens towards 6825/30, we can expect a reversion there. But the market’s main bet is a rangebound in this zone around the center. As said in the weekly post, any move is unlikely today.
